resources.string: Open System Settings · Language & input · Manage keyboards, then enable Nom Keyboard
Tap here to show the input method picker and switch to Nom Keyboard
Type here to try it out…
Use a dark background for the keyboard
Choose traditional (hóa, thúy) or modern (hoá, thuý) placement on open diphthongs such as oa, oe, uy
How the space key handles the current composing text: segment-by-segment, commit-when-no-candidate, or always commit directly
When you have typed only one syllable (or part of one), show single-character Nom candidates only; multi-character words will appear only once you have entered enough syllables

When what you\'ve typed cannot be a normal Vietnamese syllable (e.g. nhma, nch), the keyboard auto-splits it into syllable prefixes (nh\'ma, n\'ch) and suggests compound words that match segment-by-segment, as well as single-character candidates for the first segment.
How many consecutive Nom characters the keyboard learns to give smarter context-aware suggestions
Save everything the keyboard has learned (frequencies of Nom character sequences) to a TSV file for backup or transfer to another device
Load n-gram frequencies from a previously exported TSV file; can be merged with current data or fully replace it
Merge the new frequencies with the current data (counts are summed), or replace all current data?
Light haptic feedback when pressing keys
Play a click sound when pressing keys
Show Nom candidates above the keyboard, tap to commit
Adjust the font size of the main keyboard keys and the suggestion bar
Turn Shift on automatically at the start of a sentence or after a full stop
Render Nom characters in the suggestion bar and test-input box using the bundled Han-Nom Gothic font. Turn off to follow the system font.
Forget recently used Nom character frequencies
Delete every entry in the user dictionary? This action cannot be undone.
No entries yet. Tap “Add” to create the first one, or “Import” to load a TSV file.
Please enter at least one Nom character
Merge new entries with the current dictionary, or replace everything?
